title: Design and in vitro transcription of shRNA with the NEB HiScribe™ T7 Quick High Yield RNA Synthesis Kit
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"eme31","text":"This a protocol for design and in vitro transcription (IVT) of shRNA with T7 RNA polymerase from a dsDNA template created by annealing two complementary ssDNA oligos. A single reaction should produce between 30 and 150 µg shRNA depending on the sequence directly downstream of the T7 promoter (and to some part probably the overall sequence). The protocol was used to produce shRNA to target different mRNAs for RNAi-based knockdown after electroporation into Aiptasia zygotes. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":31,"length":8}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"5fbb3","text":"The template generation, in vitro transcription and purification span over two days (overnight in vitro transcription) and should be followed in one go, delays or freeze-thaw cycles will lead to an increased amount of RNA degradation (independently from RNases).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":262},{"style":"italic","offset":25,"length":8},{"style":"italic","offset":94,"length":9}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"72i94","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2fv8c","text":"This protocol uses annealed ssDNA oligonucleotides as template for in vitro transcription via the NEB HiScribe™ T7 Quick High Yield RNA Synthesis Kit. The resulting shRNA should be purified, QC'ed and quantified, single-use aliquoted and stored at −80°C immediately following this protocol. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":98,"length":53}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fvc9p","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ms7u","text":"Introduction of foreign RNA can have an effect on its own on an organism, so it is highly important to include appropriate controls: The gold-standard for shRNA are ones with so-called scrambled sequences which have the exact same but randomized nucleotide composition (and general features of high-efficiency shRNAs) as the target-specific ones without targets in the organism. Other, less ideal options are any shRNAs without targets in the organism. It is recommended to check transcript levels via RT-qPCR or other means to confirm knockdown of the target, in case of suspected off-targets, including them as well is advisable.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":132}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"d7es3","text":"Use only RNase-free reagents and consumables and follow general rules for the work with RNA such as use of regularly changed gloves, treatment of any surfaces touched during the extraction with RNase decontamination solution (e.g., RNase Away or RNaseZAP) and keeping the work area clean and tidy. NEB has a nice guide here: https://www.neb.com/en-us/tools-and-resources/usage-guidelines/avoiding-ribonuclease-contamination","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":325,"length":98}],"data":{}},{"key":"bk9tv","text":"Keep the RNA on ice unless otherwise noted, once final capped mRNA purified and eluted into nuclease-free water, keep it on ice and finish quality control, quantification and make aliquots in one go to store the aliquots at −80°C. Having the RNA out on ice longer than necessary increases the risk of degradation. Degradation will also occur from freeze-thaw cycles (hence the single-use aliquots).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"2C08B643423211F0AB870A58A9FEAC02","url":"https://www.neb.com/en-us/tools-and-resources/usage-guidelines/avoiding-ribonuclease-contamination"}}}}

steps: Check Exaiptasia diaphana target gene annotation on NCBI for correctly annotated coding sequence (CDS; exons without introns and UTRs) with the RNAseq-coverage track and correct exons manually if necessary. Save the correct CDS as a FASTA file. Plain link address: https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/gdv/?org=exaiptasia-diaphana
Mix exactly 5 µL of the 50 µM sense oligo and 5 µL of the 50 µM anti-sense oligo in a PCR tube.
Denature for 5 min at 98°C in a thermocycler.

Remove the DNA from the cycler and let it slowly cool down at RT for ≥10 min. Keep at RT or place on ice until proceeding with the in vitro transcription. This results in a 25 µM dsDNA corresponding to ~1 µg/µL of the 66-bp template. We always use freshly annealed DNA as IVT template but it should be no issue storing it at −20°C for multiple uses.

Set up a 30-µL in vitro transcription reaction in the following order in a nuclease-free PCR tube at RT: 14.8 µL nuclease-free water 0.7 µL RNase inhibitor, murine 10 µL NTP/Buffer mix 1.5 µL DTT (100 mM) 1 µL template dsDNA (corresponding to ~1 µg DNA) 2 µL T7 polymerase mix
Incubate at 37°C for 12-16 h (overnight). It might be beneficial for the yield to mix the reaction every 30 min for the first 1-2 h but we haven't tested that.
Add 30 µL nuclease-free water and 2 µL DNaseI to the reactions and mix well.
Incubate for 15-30 min at 37°C.
Place reactions on ice until continuing with purification.

Purify the shRNA with one of the two accompanying protocols for the Zymo Research RNA Clean & Concentrator™-25 Kit (for 1-3 IVT reactions depending on the yield) or Zymo Research Quick-RNA Miniprep Kit (for larger numbers of reactions).
Add 1.5 µL nuclease-free water into a pre-chilled RNase-free PCR tube on ice.

Use the prepared sample to check RNA size and integrity by gel electrophoresis. A massive band at ~50 nt is expected. We usually use either 2.5% agarose in TAE+bleach gels or in sodium borate+bleach and standard DNA ladders and loading dyes (allows to see the degradation and get a rough estimate of size) or for a better size assessment (e.g., when a new construct has been transcribed the first time) use standard 2.5% agarose in sodium borate gels (without bleach) but in combination with denaturing RNA loading dye and an RNA ladder in the same dye. RNA agarose electrophoresis with bleach gels RNA agarose electrophoresis with denaturing RNA sample buffer
Transfer 1 µL of the mix into a new PCR tube and prepare for electrophoresis, keep on ice. This is to check on RNA integrity and size.
Add 1.5 µL of the RNA solution to the water, mix by pipetting and put on ice.
Use Invivogen's siRNA wizard platform to find target sites. Plain link address: https://www.invivogen.com/sirnawizard/design.php
In the "Find siRNA sequences" tab, enter the target name and CDS.
Set the motif size to 19 nt and click "search".

Select 2-5 of the resulting sense sequences. The criteria are (most of them are taken care of by the software): A GC content of 40-55%. Low GC content at the 3′ end. End with an A or T (highlighted blue in the search results). No or minimal low-complexity parts. Start with a G at the 5′ end (This is absolutely required for T7 RNA polymeerase activity and also highly beneficial for RNAi activity). For more efficient transcription from the T7 promoter, two Gs at the 5′ end are preferential. If absolutely no other good quality sequences for a target can be found starting with a least one G, add one to the T7 promoter sequence (this might result in a less active shRNA).

BLAST the sense sequences against the Exaiptasia diaphana transcriptome on NCBI: Use the following options: Standard databases (nr etc.) Reference RNA sequences (refseq_rna) Organism: Exaiptasia diaphana (taxid:2652724) Keep the defaults for all other options Discard sequences which have additional hits with less than four mismatches or more than 16 contiguous identical nucleotides with other transcripts, especially at the 5′ end of the anti-sense sequence. Alternatively , use Bowtie v1.3.1 set to three mismatches allowed (-n 3) to search for hits against the Aiptasia transcriptome, do not use sequences with multiple hits detected for at least 14 contiguous identical nucleotides. For a more stringent selection , discard sequences with less than six mismatches (-n 5 in Bowtie) and more than 14 continuous identical nucleotides.
Once you found a set of two sequences without any likely off-target hits, save them in a text editor and produce scrambled control sequences for them:

Switch to the " Scramble siRNA " tab, enter the target-specific sequence and name and click "Scramble". This creates a randomized sequence with the same nucleotide composition as the original and the same criteria as for the target-specific one. Unfortunately, it does not allow checking custom mRNA databases other than human, rat and mouse and only gives one sequence, not a list of multiple candidates.

As before, check each scrambled sequence for no hits to the Aiptasia transcriptome with the criteria as in go to step #2.4 . If the sequence does not fullfil the criteria, go to the previous page and hit "Scramble" again, this will produce a different scrambled sequence. Repeat until you have suitable scrambled controls.
Now design the ssDNA oligos to order as IVT template:

In a sequence-editing program, combine the following sequence components: T7 core promoter: TAATACGACTCACTATA(G) Sense target sequence: target-specific, 19 bp Linker/hairpin loop: TTCAAGAGA anti-sense target sequence: target-specific, 19 bp (reverse-complement of sense sequence) TT overhang: TT Reverse-complement sequences can be generated here: https://www.bioinformatics.org/sms/rev_comp.html An example for targeting the mNeonGreen mRNA: T7 core promoter: taatacgactcactata Sense target sequence: GGTGCCTCCCTTACTGTTA Linker/hairpin loop: TTCAAGAGA anti-sense target sequence: TAACAGTAAGGGAGGCACC TT overhang: TT This results in the following DNA sequence for the forward oligo to order (transcribed part bold): 5′-taatacgactcactata GGTGCCTCCCTTACTGTTA TTCAAGAGA TAACAGTAAGGGAGGCACC TT -3′ The reverse-complement oligo to order: 5′- AA GGTGCCTCCCTTACTGTTA TCTCTTGAA TAACAGTAAGGGAGGCACC tatagtgagtcgtatta-3′
Do the same for all targets and scrambled sequences.

Order the oligonucleotides (66 nt or if a G needed to be added to the promoter, 67 nt) as standard, desalted lyophilized primers at 25 or 100 nmol scale. 25 nmol should be enough for plenty IVT reactions but might not be available for that length.

Check RNA folding predictions for the transcribed part (excluding T7 promoter) via RNAfold (or other computational means), if available use the Andronescu et al. (2007) method (under "Energy parameters" in RNAfold). This should result in a single hairpin structure as in the example. Plain link address: http://rna.tbi.univie.ac.at/cgi-bin/RNAWebSuite/RNAfold.cgi Example secondary-structure prediction by RNAfold for an mNeonGreen shRNA.

Reconstitute the sense and the anti-sense oligonucleotides to 50 µM concentration in 10 mM Tris-Cl, pH 8.5. Add the required volume and let stand at RT or 37°C for 15 min, mix occasionally and spin down to collect liquid in the bottom of the tubes. Store at −20°C. DNA duplex formation (annealing) requires monovalent salt ions. Synthesized oligonucleotides of standard, desalted purity grade contain enough salt. Karabulut et al. (2019) used plain nuclease-free water for reconstitution, but a slightly alkaline buffer is better for DNA long-term storage (especially with frequent freeze-thaw cycles). For a buffer with extra potassium, IDT sells Nuclease-Free Duplex Buffer (30 mM HEPES, pH 7.5; 100 mM potassium acetate) in 2-mL aliquots (11-01-03-01). We have used all three buffers and they all worked fine but we have not performed a direct comparison. If using a different buffer for reconstitution and duplexing, just be sure that it does not contain EDTA which might interfere with the down Alternatively, buffers with mono-valent salt ions are used for a more efficient duplex formation, e.g., 10 mM Tris-Cl, pH 7.5-8.5 and 20-100 mM potassium or sodium salts. Omit any EDTA as well as di-valent ions like Mg 2+ as they might interfere with the following enzymatic reactions. Some companies such as IDT offer to directly order dsDNA duplexes. Except for the added costs, there is nothing speaking against that, just be sure the buffer (if the annealed oligos are not delivered lyophilized) fulfills the criteria described above.
creator: Christian CR Renicke
